content.problemStatement:
In your house, you have a lot of integers. These integers are coloured by QQQ colours (numbered 111 through QQQ). For each valid iii, you have bib_ibi‚Äã integers with colour iii and each of these integers has value aia_iai‚Äã.

For each integer iii such that 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N, you need to find the number of different partitions of iii. Here, a partition of iii consists of one or more integers from your house such that the sum of values of these integers is equal to iii. Two partitions are different if there is a colour such that the number of integers with this colour in one partition is different from the number of integers with this colour in the other partition. Since these numbers could be very big, compute them modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: 1‚â§ai,bi‚â§N1 \le a_i, b_i \le N1‚â§ai‚Äã,bi‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
QQQ lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contain two space-separated integers aia_iai‚Äã and bib_ibi‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be the number of partitions of iii, modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
